rationale: Both candidates independently converge on the same core latent concern the ground-truth reviewer raised (dsocek's point that hardcoding pipeline component names is fragile and should derive from an existing per-pipeline declaration), though neither names the exact source (_packed_modules_mapping/stacked_params_mapping) dsocek pointed to. X goes further: it also acknowledges xuechendi's validation-before/after request, cross-references a duplicate hardcoded list in registry.py and the offloader's SupportsComponentDiscovery pattern, and raises a novel, well-grounded follow-up (text-encoder LoRA still silently no-ops) — all with precise file:line citations. Y's finding is essentially the same single nit restated with more verbose telemetry-style output and hedged, partly-unverified assumptions, and it misses the validation-request thread entirely.
